sections.heading: Architecture Overview
sections.content:
The list view is implemented in `list.js` (~520 lines). It renders `App.state.filteredNodes` as a sortable, groupable HTML table with 7 toggleable columns: Select (checkbox), Name, Type, Location, Staleness, Tags, Code (preview).

**Core state** lives on the `List` object:
- `_sortCol` / `_sortDir` — current sort column and direction
- `_groupBy` — null (flat) or one of: `node_type`, `subtype`, `location`, `staleness`, `tag`
- `_selectedIds` (Set) — checkbox-selected node IDs for bulk operations
- `_collapsedGroups` (Set) — which group headers are collapsed
- `_columns` (Object) — column visibility map (`{check: true, title: true, …, code: false}`)
- `_causeCache` — staleness-cause API results cached per node ID
- `_previewCache` — 3-line source snippets cached per node ID
- Monaco state (`_monacoEditor`, `_focusMode`, `_currentSourceData`, etc.)

**Rendering flow:** The table re-renders from scratch on every filter, sort, group, or column change. Nodes are first sorted, then bucketed by `_groupNodes()` into collapsible groups (each with a header row showing label + count). Column visibility is applied per-cell during row construction. For <2000 nodes this is fast enough; virtual scrolling is deferred until projects exceed 5000.

**Sidebar integration:** Two list-only sidebar sections (`.list-only-section`) are toggled visible/hidden in `App.setView()`:
- **Group By** — radio selector that calls `List.setGroupBy()`
- **Actions** — Run Check, Verify Selected, View in Graph buttons

**Code preview (3 tiers):**
- Tier 1 (inline): Optional "Code" column shows 3-line monospace snippet, lazy-loaded from `/api/nodes/{id}/source` with client-side caching.
- Tier 2 (panel): "View Source" link opens a bottom-split Monaco panel. Own instance, independent of Workflow/Tests.
- Tier 3 (focus): Toggle switches between full file (highlighted range) and isolated function view (offset line numbers preserving original file positions).

**Spawn graph:** `viewInGraph()` collects selected or filtered node IDs, filters `App.state.allEdges` to only connecting edges (client-side), loads via `Graph.loadAll()`, and switches to graph view. No backend endpoint required.

sections.heading: Key Interactions
sections.content:
```
List.render(nodes, stalenessMap):
  1. Store nodes + staleness, prune stale selections
  2. Update toolbar: "N nodes" or "K of N selected"
  3. _renderRows():
     a. Sort by _sortCol / _sortDir
     b. _groupNodes() → [{key, label, nodes}] (flat if _groupBy is null)
     c. For each group:
        - Render group header <tr> with toggle/label/count (if grouped)
        - Skip rows if group is collapsed
        - For each node: build <tr> with cells gated by _columns visibility
     d. Update sort indicators on <th> elements
     e. Sync select-all checkbox state

List.setGroupBy(field):
  1. Update _groupBy, clear collapsed state
  2. Re-render rows

List.openSource(nodeId):
  1. Show #list-source-panel, fetch /api/nodes/{id}/source
  2. If Monaco ready → _renderMonaco(data)
  3. Else queue as _pendingSource, render placeholder

List.toggleFocus():
  1. Flip _focusMode flag
  2. Re-render Monaco with either full content or sliced focus range
  3. In focus mode: line numbers offset to match original file positions

List.viewInGraph():
  1. Collect IDs from _selectedIds (or all filteredNodes)
  2. Filter allEdges to edges with both endpoints in set
  3. Graph.loadAll(subNodes, subEdges, …)
  4. App.setView('graph')

Column toggle:
  1. User clicks "Columns" dropdown or "{ } Code" shortcut
  2. toggleColumn(colKey, visible) → update _columns, sync <th> hidden class
  3. Re-render rows (cells gated by _columns)
```

sections.heading: Implementation Trade-offs
sections.content:
### Full DOM rebuild vs virtual scrolling
The table rebuilds all rows on every re-render. For <2000 nodes this is imperceptible. Virtual scrolling would add complexity for marginal gain. If projects exceed 5000 nodes, pagination should be added.

### Shared staleness CSS
Staleness badges use the same `.staleness-badge.DESC_UPDATED` etc. classes as everywhere else, ensuring consistent color coding across views.

### No sidebar file fork
The PRD suggested forking `sidebar-graph.js` / `sidebar-list.js`. Instead, list-only sections use a `.list-only-section` CSS class toggled in `App.setView()`. This avoids throwaway work since the frontend is planned for a TypeScript migration.

### Client-side graph spawn (no /api/subgraph)
The PRD specified a `POST /api/subgraph` endpoint. Since `App.state.allEdges` is already loaded, edge filtering is done client-side in `viewInGraph()`. This avoids a new endpoint and round-trip for the common case.

### Own Monaco instance
The list source panel uses its own Monaco instance, independent of Workflow and Tests. All three register via the same CDN AMD loader but maintain separate `_monacoReady` / `_monacoEditor` state. This avoids cross-view disposal issues.

### Lazy-loaded code previews
Tier 1 snippets are fetched on first render of the Code column, not eagerly. The `/api/nodes/{id}/source` response is cached per node ID. This avoids a burst of API calls when the Code column is toggled on for hundreds of rows — only visible rows trigger fetches during scroll.

### Column visibility via cell gating
Rather than hiding columns via CSS `display:none` on `<td>` elements (which breaks `colspan` in group headers), column visibility is applied during row construction — cells for hidden columns are simply not appended. The `_visibleColCount()` method provides the correct colspan for group headers.

sections.heading: Hierarchical Focus Traversal
sections.content:
For docjson nodes with nested sections, the source panel's binary Focus toggle is replaced with directional Up/Down navigation buttons.

**Behavior:**
- **Up (↑):** Navigate from the current section to its parent section. If already at a top-level section, zoom out to show the full document. If already showing the full doc, the button is disabled.
- **Down (↓):** Navigate from the current section to its first child section. If the section has no children, the button is disabled. From the full doc view, Down navigates to the first top-level section.
- No lateral (sibling) movement — the selected node determines the vertical axis. To view a sibling, select it from the list.

**Data source:**
- The `/api/docs/{id}/render` response includes `parent_id` and `depth` per section, which the frontend uses to build the traversal chain
- The source panel caches the section tree for the current document so Up/Down navigation is instant (no API calls)

**Non-docjson nodes:**
- Functions, tests, and modules keep the existing binary Focus toggle (full file vs isolated function). The Up/Down buttons only appear when a docjson section or document node is selected.

**UI:**
- Two small arrow buttons (↑ ↓) replace the Focus toggle in `#list-source-controls` when a docjson node is selected
- A breadcrumb trail shows the current position in the hierarchy: `Doc Title > Parent Section > Current Section`
- Disabled buttons are grayed out with a tooltip explaining why (e.g., "Already at top level", "No child sections")

**Interactions:**
```
List.navigateUp():
  1. If current view is a section with parent_id → load parent section content
  2. If current view is a top-level section → switch to full doc rendered view
  3. If current view is full doc → button disabled (no-op)
  4. Update breadcrumb, re-render source panel

List.navigateDown():
  1. If current view is full doc → load first top-level section
  2. If current section has children → load first child section
  3. If current section is a leaf → button disabled (no-op)
  4. Update breadcrumb, re-render source panel
```

sections.heading: Technical Verification
sections.content:
- Sort by each column in ascending and descending order
- Click a row and verify the detail panel opens with correct node data
- Filter by type in sidebar and verify the count label updates
- Staleness badges match the colors from `/api/check`
- Group by each option (type, subtype, module, staleness, tag) and verify headers show correct counts
- Collapse and expand groups; verify row visibility toggles
- Select rows via checkboxes; verify toolbar count updates
- Select all → verify all rows checked; deselect → verify cleared
- Click stale badge → tooltip shows cause from `/api/nodes/{id}/staleness-cause`
- Click row verify button → node transitions to VERIFIED after prompt
- Bulk verify selected → all selected nodes transition; selection clears
- "Run Check" button → staleness refreshes in place; tooltip cache clears
- Toggle Code column via toolbar or Columns menu → preview cells appear/disappear
- Code preview loads 3-line snippet from correct focus range
- Click "View Source" → bottom Monaco panel opens with file content
- Focus Function toggle → Monaco shows only function lines with offset numbering
- Drag resize handle on source panel → panel height adjusts
- Close source panel → Monaco disposed, panel hidden
- "View in Graph" with selection → graph loads only selected nodes + connecting edges
- "View in Graph" without selection → graph loads all filtered nodes
- Location column links open VS Code at correct file and line
- Column menu → toggle each column → table re-renders with correct columns
